plot_frame_box.description: Plot interior bounded by leftmost tick col 66 ('10,000 BCE'), rightmost tick col 720 ('2023'), top row 50 (just below title band), bottom row 512 (x-axis line). No vertical y-axis line is drawn by OWID; left bound anchored on the first labeled tick.

pixels_per_coordinate_unit.x_unit_label: year (BCE encoded as negative; '0' = year 0; '2023' = CE year)
pixels_per_coordinate_unit.y_unit_label: people (raw count; tick label 'N billion' = N * 1e9 people)

x_axis.formula: px_col = m * year + b (linear; BCE negative)

y_axis.formula: px_row = m * people + b (linear; raw people)
y_axis.convention_note: Tick labels '1 billion'..'8 billion' converted by multiplying the number by 1e9; '0' kept as 0. CSV y values are stored in raw people.

worked_example.scenario: Locate (year=1700, population=0.5e9 people) in pixel space to sanity-check the formulas.
worked_example.verification: Col ~703 (between the '0' tick at 610 and the '2023' tick at 720) and row ~483 (about 27 px above the axis at 510, which is 27/54 ~ 0.5 billion). Both consistent with the chart.

detection_internals.y_label_band_cols: 0-65 ('N billion' tick label text on the left)
detection_internals.extraction_method: Per-pixel winner-take-all classification: each pixel inside the plot is assigned to the series whose reference BGR is closest (Euclidean), if within distance 45 AND the nearest beats the second-nearest by at least 5. Per column, the chosen row is the median of the matched pixel cluster, except: 'World' uses the topmost cluster (highest line) and 'Oceania' uses the bottommost cluster (lowest line). Gaps are filled by carrying the previous matched row forward; rows before any match default to the axis row (y=0).
detection_internals.x_axis_convention: BCE years stored as negative numerics. '10,000 BCE' -> -10000, '8,000 BCE' -> -8000, ..., '0' -> 0, '2023' -> 2023.
detection_internals.y_axis_convention: SI-suffix tick labels expanded: '1 billion' -> 1e9 people, '8 billion' -> 8e9. CSV y values are raw people counts.
